rows.praised: Default OpenAI-compatible multi-provider proxy; unmatched model breadth
rows.gripe: Latency/memory overhead and degradation at high RPS
rows.event: ⚠️ PyPI supply-chain attack (Mar 2026) — v1.82.7/1.82.8 were backdoored with a credential stealer after a Trivy CI-token compromise (TeamPCP); PyPI quarantined the project in ~3h. Pin to a clean release. Trend Micro · Datadog
rows.search: LiteLLM Default OpenAI-compatible multi-provider proxy; unmatched model breadth Latency/memory overhead and degradation at high RPS ⚠️ PyPI supply-chain attack (Mar 2026) — v1.82.7/1.82.8 were backdoored with a credential stealer after a Trivy CI-token compromise (TeamPCP); PyPI quarantined the project in ~3h. Pin to a clean release. Trend Micro · Datadog

rows.praised: 400+ models behind one key and one bill; hard spend caps
rows.gripe: ~5.5% credit fee at scale; provider quality/quantization varies; no public SLA
rows.event: ~50-min database outage (Aug 28 2025), then two more on Feb 17 & 19 2026 — all DB-layer failures, not upstream providers (the Feb pair: the caching layer dropped every DB connection, returning 401s with ~80–90% request failures); ~99.96% uptime over 8 months, but no SLA or downtime credits. StatusGator · Feb 2026 postmortem
rows.search: OpenRouter 400+ models behind one key and one bill; hard spend caps ~5.5% credit fee at scale; provider quality/quantization varies; no public SLA ~50-min database outage (Aug 28 2025), then two more on Feb 17 & 19 2026 — all DB-layer failures, not upstream providers (the Feb pair: the caching layer dropped every DB connection, returning 401s with ~80–90% request failures); ~99.96% uptime over 8 months, but no SLA or downtime credits. StatusGator · Feb 2026 postmortem

rows.praised: Genuinely production-grade; strong observability, fallbacks, prompt management
rows.gripe: Much "review" content is SEO — weigh G2/Gartner over listicles
rows.event: Acquired by Palo Alto Networks (closed May 2026) — now the control plane for Prisma AIRS; a neutrality/roadmap question if you want vendor-independent infra. Palo Alto Networks
rows.search: Portkey Genuinely production-grade; strong observability, fallbacks, prompt management Much "review" content is SEO — weigh G2/Gartner over listicles Acquired by Palo Alto Networks (closed May 2026) — now the control plane for Prisma AIRS; a neutrality/roadmap question if you want vendor-independent infra. rows.praised: One endpoint, 0% markup on BYOK, native to the AI SDK
rows.event: Vercel breach (Apr 2026) — a Context.ai OAuth supply-chain compromise exposed environment variables for a subset of customers. Not the gateway product itself, but it bears on trusting Vercel with your keys. TechCrunch · Vercel
rows.search: Vercel AI Gateway One endpoint, 0% markup on BYOK, native to the AI SDK Durable independent sentiment still thin Vercel breach (Apr 2026) — a Context.ai OAuth supply-chain compromise exposed environment variables for a subset of customers. Not the gateway product itself, but it bears on trusting Vercel with your keys. TechCrunch · Vercel

rows.praised: Zero-infra observability / caching / rate-limiting on CF's edge; dollar spend limits
rows.event: Newer entrant; independent production-review depth is still thin.
rows.search: Cloudflare AI Gateway Zero-infra observability / caching / rate-limiting on CF's edge; dollar spend limits Limited request-placement / routing control Newer entrant; independent production-review depth is still thin.

rows.praised: Inherits a battle-tested data plane + broad plugin ecosystem
rows.gripe: Open-core: several important plugins are Enterprise-only (some prefer Apache APISIX for predictable OSS)
rows.event: The AI Gateway is newer; most Kong-vs-LiteLLM/Portkey benchmarks are vendor-published — reproduce before trusting.

rows.event: ⚠️ Archived June 2026 — company wound down; Apache-2.0 code and community forks remain, but plan for self-support, not a vendor roadmap. GitHub

rows.praised: ~2-minute onboarding; fast cost/usage and token-limit debugging
rows.gripe: As a proxy it sits in the request path (a SPOF unless you use async logging)
rows.event: Acquired by Mintlify (Mar 2026), now maintenance mode — security fixes, bug fixes and new models keep shipping; Mintlify supports migration. Mintlify · Helicone

rows.praised: Markets itself as a fast Go drop-in for LiteLLM
rows.gripe: Independent production evidence is still thin
rows.event: Its 50–90× lower-p99 claims are vendor-reported — reproduce on your own workload before betting on them.
